name: Four Seasons Resort Sharm El Sheikh

content.verdict: The Four Seasons sits on a private cove well outside the Sharm strip, cascading down a hillside in a tumble of villas and Moorish-domed buildings to a quiet beach where the house reef begins ten metres from the loungers. For honeymooners who want world-class diving and snorkeling without giving up Four Seasons polish — and who would rather have a quiet villa than a buzzing party hotel — this is the smartest beach choice in Egypt.
content.best_room: Aim for a One-Bedroom Royal Suite with private terrace and pool, or one of the new hilltop villas if budget allows — both deliver the iconic stepped Red Sea view that the 270 standard rooms only partially see. If staying in the main building, request a Premier Room high in Building 1 for the cleanest sea panorama.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & house reef snorkel
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Sharm El Sheikh (SSH) — the resort is twenty minutes from the airport and sends a private car. Take the funicular down to the beach, claim a cabana, and slip a mask on for your first look at the house reef: parrotfish, butterflyfish, and the inner edge of the wall start in chest-deep water. Casual sunset dinner at Reef Grill on the beach, early to bed.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa welcome & long pool day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Late breakfast, then a couples' welcome ritual at the Albatros Spa: hammam scrub, oud-and-frankincense oil massage in adjoining suites, herbal tea on the relaxation terrace. Long afternoon at the upper infinity pool with sea views, a few laps and a long siesta. Dinner at Il Frantoio — the wood-fired branzino and the Bekaa Valley wine list deserve a night.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Ras Mohammed snorkel & dive day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full-day boat charter to Ras Mohammed National Park — Shark Reef, Yolanda Reef, and Anemone City are some of the best snorkel and dive sites in the world. The dive centre at the resort is excellent for beginners (intro dives) and certified divers (drift dives along the wall). Back at sunset, drinks at the Arabesque Lounge with the brass shisha and oud playing.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Desert quad-bike & Bedouin dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Mid-morning private 4x4 transfer to a Bedouin camp in the Sinai desert for a quad-bike ride across the dunes, camel sunset, and a slow grilled-lamb dinner under the stars with traditional music. The contrast between the reef in the morning and the desert at night is one of the great Sinai pleasures. Back at the resort by 10pm.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Slow day & private dhow sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Truly do nothing until 4pm — long breakfast in your room, beach all morning, lunch at AQUA poolside. Late afternoon, the concierge sets up a private dhow charter for the two of you with a chef on board, sailing along the coast with mezze, grilled prawns, and a bottle of cold rosé. Back at the marina at dark.

itinerary_7_nights.title: St. Catherine's & Mount Sinai (optional dawn)
itinerary_7_nights.description: Either an early-morning hike up Mount Sinai for sunrise (departing the night before, returning the same evening — strenuous but unforgettable), or a gentler day trip to the 6th-century Saint Catherine's Monastery and the Burning Bush site. If you skip both, take a half-day desert spa picnic on the property instead and use the day to recover.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell breakfast above the Red Sea
itinerary_7_nights.description: Long breakfast on the terrace at Arabesque, then a final reef snorkel and a quick poolside lunch before transfer. The hotel offers a late-checkout day-use room for honeymooners on request — worth it for a shower and a quick dinner before the flight back to Cairo or Europe.

caveats: The resort is built on a steep hillside and you rely on funiculars and golf-cart shuttles to reach the beach — fine for most couples but worth knowing if mobility is a concern.
It is family-friendly with a kids' club and pool, so school holidays (Christmas, Easter, August) bring children — request the upper adults-leaning pool for quieter swims.
Sharm El Sheikh is a charter-tourism destination overall; flights from Europe arrive packed and visa lines at the airport can be slow — pre-arrange the meet-and-greet service through the hotel.
The Red Sea has occasional jellyfish blooms and rare shark advisories — usually addressed quickly but check with the dive centre before any free swim.
Alcohol is served but discreet by Egyptian rules; expect mid-range wines marked up sharply versus Europe.

faqs.question: Is it good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — it consistently ranks as the most romantic Red Sea resort thanks to the cove location, the house reef, and the suite-with-pool category. Honeymoon perks include sparkling wine on arrival, room upgrade subject to availability, and a complimentary couples' ritual at the spa on stays of five nights or more.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: March to May and September to November are the sweet spots — water temperature is ideal for snorkeling, daytime air is 25–30°C, and crowds thinner than peak winter. December–February is great for sun but cooler water (low 20s).

faqs.question: Should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Yes for villas and Royal Suites — they book six months ahead for the November–March high season. Standard rooms are usually available 30–60 days out outside school holidays.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, children are welcome and there is a kids' club. Demographic outside school holidays skews adult and couples-heavy, however; book May/June or late September for the quietest atmosphere.

faqs.question: Best room type for couples?
faqs.answer: A One-Bedroom Royal Suite with sea-view plunge pool is the honeymoon pick. If saving, a Premier Room high in Building 1 has the cleanest standard-category view.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Direct flights to Sharm El Sheikh (SSH) from London, Paris, Frankfurt, Milan, and most major Gulf hubs. The hotel transfer is twenty minutes. Visa on arrival is straightforward; pre-arrange the resort's meet-and-greet to skip queues.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Four Seasons Resort Sharm El Sheikh

Dear team,

We are planning our honeymoon and are interested in staying from [DATE] to [DATE]. Could you advise on availability of a One-Bedroom Royal Suite with sea-view pool, any honeymoon packages, and dive-package add-ons for two (one beginner, one certified)?

Thank you,
[Names]
